creator: trashcan@ellael.org
text:
(In reply to Po-Chuan Hsieh from comment #5)

This is weird ;-) 

Now, I can compile protobuf and protbuf-c without any issues, and I can reproduce the failures reported above.


What I did:

1) wipe ccache and all packages as a clean start
   compile all packages with poudriere without pre-fetching and make.conf modifications

result: all packages compiled without any issue.


2) wipe cache and all packages as a clean start
   compile all packages with poudriere with pre-fetching (besides protobuf and protobuf-c) and without make.conf modifications

result: protobuf failed with error message:

ld: error: undefined reference due to --no-allow-shlib-undefined: absl::lts_20240722::log_internal::LogMessage& absl::lts_20240722::log_internal::LogMessage::operator<<<int, 0>(int const&)
>>> referenced by libprotoc.so.27.3.0

ld: error: undefined reference due to --no-allow-shlib-undefined: absl::lts_20240722::log_internal::LogMessage& absl::lts_20240722::log_internal::LogMessage::operator<<<unsigned long, 0>(unsigned long const&)
>>> referenced by libprotoc.so.27.3.0


note: protobuf-c has been skipped, due to the failed build of protobuf.


3) modify make.conf as mentioned above, made poudriere compile both ports successfully.


Let me know if you need poudriere's logfiles for the failed and successful protobuf compilation.


Regards,
Michael


BTW: This is FreeBSD 14.1-STABLE stable/14-n268489-1d0920986f0b
